entries.title: Vercel Deployment Preview Comment Collector
entries.notes: Scrapes GitHub PR comments tied to a Vercel deployment URL and overlays them on the preview page in a side panel. Buildable in ~3 weeks using Vercel deploy hooks + GitHub REST API. Existing Vercel-adjacent tools focus on perf and status, not reviewer conversation threading.
entries.pain: Reviewers leave scattered PR comments but the Vercel preview has no native way to thread them back to the deployment.

entries.title: Shopify Customer Review Authenticity Scorer
entries.notes: Analyzes review metadata, language patterns, and reviewer history to score authenticity per product page. Side panel overlay on any Shopify storefront. Competitor gap: Yotpo/Judge.me focus on collecting reviews, not auditing integrity before launch.
entries.pain: Shopify merchants can't tell which product reviews are genuine versus paid/imported junk that tanks conversion trust.

entries.title: Linear Issue Acceptance Criteria Coverage Checker
entries.notes: Parses Linear issue descriptions and child issues, flags missing edge cases, ambiguity markers, and untestable criteria in a side panel. Sales motion: per-team pricing for product orgs. Builds on Linear's growing enterprise adoption through 2025-2026.
entries.pain: Linear issues ship to engineers with vague or missing acceptance criteria, leading to rework and 'that's not what I meant' PR ping-pong.

entries.title: Browser Web Notification Sender Verifier
entries.notes: Inspects the service worker registration chain on every web push and shows a sender-origin badge next to the OS notification. Differentiator: Browser Notification Priority Sorter (559) sorts by importance; this verifies identity. Stripe tip jar model fits the consumer trust-tool niche.
entries.pain: Web push notifications look identical but can originate from spoofed or compromised origins, fooling users into phishing taps.

entries.title: Salesforce Opportunity Stakeholder Change Tracker
entries.notes: Monitors OpportunityContactRole history via Salesforce REST API and posts alerts when stakeholder composition shifts mid-cycle. Buildable in 2-3 weeks. Complementary to existing close-date slippage and deal-stage aging tools that don't surface people changes.
entries.pain: AEs miss when a champion leaves a deal or a new economic buyer shows up, both critical signals for forecast calls.

entries.title: Adobe Analytics Workspace Adoption Tracker
entries.notes: Pulls Adobe Analytics admin logs and ranks projects by usage, dwell time, and export frequency; surfaces a leaderboard while users are in the AA UI. Niche but high willingness-to-pay because it justifies platform spend to CMOs.
entries.pain: Analytics teams build dozens of Workspace projects nobody opens, but leadership has no data on actual report consumption.

entries.title: Microsoft Clarity Session Heatmap Comparator
entries.notes: Uses Clarity API to align heatmaps by scroll depth and click region, computing delta overlays in a side panel. Free tier caps at 5 comparisons per month. Why now: Clarity has matured into a mainstream free alternative to Hotjar, increasing demand for version comparison.
entries.pain: UX designers can't easily compare Clarity heatmaps between page redesigns to prove the new version improved engagement.

entries.title: Jira Sprint Scope Creep Detector
entries.notes: Tracks Jira sprint board changes in real time on the sprint page and computes scope drift % vs committed scope at planning time. Buildable in ~2 weeks. Strong cross-sell potential into a Linear/Asana version for unified PM tooling.
entries.pain: Mid-sprint tickets silently balloon sprint scope, but standups never surface how much new work entered after planning.

entries.title: Mailchimp Campaign Subject Line A/B Winner Predictor
entries.notes: Reads the A/B setup screen in Mailchimp and scores each subject variant using historical open-rate embeddings plus an LLM judge. Freemium: 10 predictions/month. Differentiator from warmup tools: focused on creative scoring, not deliverability.
entries.pain: Email marketers can't predict which Mailchimp subject line variant will win before sending, wasting send volume on losers.

entries.title: Local Browser AI Long-Doc Summarizer
entries.notes: Uses Chrome's built-in Gemini Nano via the Prompt API to summarize articles and PDFs locally, never sending content off-device. Why now: Prompt API stabilized in Chrome 131+. Differentiator: Web Article Summarizer Pro (56) is cloud-based; this is private-by-default.
entries.pain: Long web docs and PDFs eat hours of reading time, but cloud AI summarizers leak proprietary content to vendors.

entries.title: Browser New Tab Project Context Digest
entries.notes: Replaces the new-tab page with auto-grouped session digest using URL clustering and recent-history signals. Free = last 7 days, paid = unlimited timeline and cross-device sync. Differentiator from Tab Semantic Clusterer (554): focused on brief output, not management.
entries.pain: Knowledge workers open 30+ tabs across projects and lose context every time they hit new tab, breaking deep work flow.

entries.title: Calendly Round Robin Distribution Fairness Checker
entries.notes: Scrapes Calendly admin analytics over rolling 30-day windows and computes distribution skew versus tenure, ramp status, and timezone. Per-team pricing for SDR orgs. Niche but sticky because fairness complaints are politically sensitive.
entries.pain: Sales managers suspect Calendly round-robin favors tenured reps but can't prove distribution is unfair without manual audit.

entries.title: DocuSign Envelope Field Anomaly Detector
entries.notes: Pre-flight check on the DocuSign envelope editor flags unusual field placement, missing required signers, and off-template usage. Buildable in ~3 weeks using the eSignature API. Per-seat pricing for legal ops teams handling high envelope volume.
entries.pain: Legal teams catch misconfigured DocuSign envelopes (wrong signer, missing initials) only after sending, causing rework and embarrassment.

entries.title: AWS Console Service Limit Headroom Calculator
entries.notes: Reads AWS Service Quotas via the console sidebar and overlays headroom % next to relevant resources. Free tier = 10 services tracked, paid = full coverage with alert routing. Why now: AWS Trusted Access for Service Quotas matured through 2025.
entries.pain: DevOps engineers discover they're at 95% of an AWS service limit only when a deployment fails or an API throttles.

entries.title: Gmail Receipt Auto-Categorizer for Expenses
entries.notes: Scans Gmail for receipt-like emails and extracts vendor, amount, date, and category via local LLM, exporting to CSV or QuickBooks IIF. Free = 25 receipts/month, paid unlimited with custom category rules. Strong wedge into accounting SaaS partnerships.
entries.pain: Freelancers and small biz owners dig through Gmail monthly hunting for receipts to file into expense reports.
